metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A Persian city where Persian Sapphire Iwan of the Star Garden, Covered Bazaar Beacon, Painted Tiles Sovereign Registry Court, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Ilyr-Qesh should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Ilyr-Qesh organizes power through Painted Tiles Sovereign Registry Court, public response through Covered Bazaar Beacon, civic memory through Persian Sapphire Iwan of the Star Garden, and daily life through S1 Grand Market Civic Hall, S2 Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, S3 Judicial Quarter Hall, S4 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 Refugee Commons Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Lapis Tile, Sandstone, Pale Marble Stone, Silk, White Plaster, Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces, Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Celestial Projection, and exact anchors including Painted Tiles Sovereign Registry Court, Covered Bazaar Beacon, Persian Sapphire Iwan of the Star Garden, Aid Commons, Caravanserai, Star Observatory, Grand Market Civic Hall, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook, Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A Persian civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Persian Sapphire Iwan of the Star Garden, Covered Bazaar Beacon, Painted Tiles Sovereign Registry Court, and section anchors such as Grand Market Civic Hall, Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, Judicial Quarter Hall, Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, Refugee Commons Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Ilyr-Qesh uses city code ILQ.
The Wonder is Persian Sapphire Iwan of the Star Garden.
The Beacon is Covered Bazaar Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Painted Tiles Sovereign Registry Court and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.
The source-backed section spine is: S1 Grand Market Civic Hall, S2 Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, S3 Judicial Quarter Hall, S4 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 Refugee Commons Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Ilyr-Qesh as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Ilyr-Qesh's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

forms: Persian Sapphire Iwan of the Star Garden
skyline: Persian Sapphire Iwan of the Star Garden
infrastructure: records, permits, markets, services, or rituals implied by anchors
colorLanguage: local textile, stone, metal, vegetation, or water colors from the image package

civilization.people: The population should read as Persian and city-specific: citizens occupy streets, shops, public thresholds, and landmark approaches as people who understand the local civic order.
socialClasses: laborers, carriers, messengers, and maintenance crews
households tied to local district obligations
civilization.governance: Government is centered on Painted Tiles Sovereign Registry Court. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.
civilization.religion: Religious or ritual life should only be emphasized where source anchors support it, especially names such as none explicitly dominant in the current anchors.
customs: citizens orient public life around Grand Market Civic Hall in Section 1
citizens orient public life around Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall in Section 2
citizens orient public life around Judicial Quarter Hall in Section 3
citizens orient public life around Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall in Section 4
citizens orient public life around Refugee Commons Civic Hall in Section 5
beacon changes become public behavior, not background color
exact landmark names carry social memory and directions
civic errands happen visibly at thresholds, queues, ledgers, stalls, courts, or shrines
bodyLanguage: workers move with practiced familiarity around district anchors
officials use thresholds and raised places to control attention
residents treat named landmarks as everyday orientation points
speechPatterns: public notices are tied to section anchors
local speech distinguishes civic authority, ordinary errands, and landmark obligations
people refer to D13 as the seat of power rather than a faction headquarters

streets.appearance: Streets should show Ilyr-Qesh's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Grand Market Civic Hall, S2 Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, S3 Judicial Quarter Hall, S4 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 Refugee Commons Civic Hall. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Painted Tiles Sovereign Registry Court, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
